title: hieracium villosum subsp. glabratellum
intro: Hieracium villosum subsp. glabratellum, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or woolly hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hieracium villosum subsp. glabratellum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Hieracium villosum subsp. glabratellum is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
